title: UPSC CDS 2026 — Combined Defence Services
meta_description: UPSC CDS 2026 — Officer entry into Indian Army, Navy, Air Force. IMA, Naval Academy, OTA posts. Exam pattern, salary ₹56K+, SSB interview.

hero.one_liner: Officer entry into Indian Armed Forces for graduates — join IMA, Naval Academy, Air Force Academy, or OTA through UPSC CDS exam

what_is_it: UPSC CDS (Combined Defence Services) examination is conducted twice annually to recruit graduates for officer-level entry into the Indian Military Academy (IMA — Army), Naval Academy, Air Force Academy, and Officers Training Academy (OTA). CDS I 2026 written exam is scheduled for April 12, 2026, with 451 vacancies announced. It's the PRIMARY path for graduates to become military officers without going through NDA (which is for 12th-pass candidates).

CDS has two selection stages: Written Exam (UPSC-conducted) testing English, General Knowledge, and Elementary Mathematics; and SSB Interview (5-day personality assessment, group tasks, individual tests). The written exam is relatively straightforward (high school level) — the real challenge is SSB, which eliminates ~70% of candidates through group discussions, obstacle courses, and psychological evaluation.

IMA and OTA entries are open to graduates of ANY stream (Arts, Commerce, Science, Law, etc.). Naval Academy requires Engineering degree. Air Force Academy requires Bachelor's degree with Physics + Mathematics at 12th level. OTA offers Short Service Commission (14 years) while IMA, Naval Academy, and AFA offer Permanent Commission (entire service). After commissioning, officers start at rank of Lieutenant with basic pay ₹56,100/month.

CDS is considered an elite examination due to the prestige of military service. Officers get: Permanent employment, generous leave, international exposure, medical benefits for family, pension, and opportunity to serve the nation. The entry salary is identical to IAS (₹56,100 basic), but military life involves unique challenges and structured career progression.

key_details.label: IMA/OTA Education
key_details.value: Any Bachelor's degree (any stream) with 50%+ marks

key_details.label: Air Force Academy Education
key_details.value: Bachelor's with Physics + Maths at 12th level (or BE/BTech). 50%+ marks.

key_details.label: Age Limit
key_details.value: IMA/AFA: 20-25 years. Naval: 19-25 years. OTA: 20-27 years.

key_details.label: Gender
key_details.value: IMA/Naval/AFA: Men only. OTA: Men AND Women (first time 2024).

exam_pattern.tier_name: Written Exam — English, GK, Math (6 hours total, 3 papers)
exam_pattern.description: Three papers of 2 hours each. Total 300 marks (100+100+100). OTA candidates write only 2 papers (English + GK, 200 marks; no Math). Standard 10th-12th class difficulty level.

extra_sections.heading: CDS vs NDA — Complete Comparison for Officer Aspirants
content: If You Missed NDA During 12th (CDS is Your Second Chance)
NDA is for 12th-pass candidates (16.5-19.5 years). If you're now 20-22 years with a Bachelor's degree, CDS is your entry into military service as an officer.
Don't worry about missing NDA — CDS-entry officers eventually reach the same positions (General, Air Chief Marshal, Naval Commander), though they start junior by 1-2 years.
Training & Officer Development
NDA: 3 years of military training at academy + 1 year specialized training = 4 years total before commission. You get a FREE degree during NDA.
CDS (IMA): 18 months of intensive officer training + immediately commissioned. CDS (OTA): 49 weeks quick officer training + 14-year commitment.
OTA is the fastest path to officer rank.
Key Differences
Exam Difficulty: CDS written exam is EASIER than NDA (no advanced mathematics, simpler GK). SSB interview is identical — equally challenging.
Seniority: NDA-entry officers are senior to CDS-entry by ~1-2 years of service. Both paths lead to same ranks eventually.
Career Path: NDA = Lifelong permanent commission. CDS OTA = 14 years SSC with option to convert to permanent.
Why Choose CDS Over NDA?
You already have a degree (age advantage, maturity). CDS is easier to crack than NDA (fewer competitors, simpler syllabus).
OTA is fastest route to officer commission (49 weeks vs 4 years for NDA).

extra_sections.heading: SSB Interview — The Real Challenge
content: What is SSB?

Service Selection Board (SSB) is a 5-day intensive interview conducted by Indian Armed Forces. It's not a traditional 'Q&A' interview — it's a personality and aptitude assessment through: Group discussions, individual presentations, outdoor obstacle courses, psychometric tests, formal interview.
About 70% of candidates are rejected at SSB; clearing written exam ≠ selection.
Five Days of Assessment
Day 1: Screening (Officer Like Qualities verbal test + written matrices). Candidates scoring low here are 'released' (rejected).
Day 2-4: Group tasks (group discussion, outdoor group tasks like bridge building, command tasks). Assessors watch: leadership, decision-making, teamwork, communication.
Day 5: Final interview (personal background, career goals, psychological evaluation). Officers assess your suitability as a military officer.
Officer Like Qualities (OLQs) Assessed

Officer Intelligence (analytical thinking), Logical Reasoning, Emotional Maturity (handling stress), Courage (moral + physical), Leadership (influencing others), Teamwork, Adaptability, Awareness (general knowledge), Communication (clear expression), Motivation (why military). SSB rewards: Balanced personality, good communication, leadership potential, emotional stability.
Preparation Strategy

(1) Start physical fitness 6 months before SSB (running, obstacle practice). (2) Develop opinions on national issues (military, politics, economy, foreign relations). (3) Practice group discussions — speak clearly, listen actively, don't dominate.
(4) Read biographies of military leaders. (5) Work on your story (why you want to serve, military family background, etc.). (6) Mock SSB interviews with coaching institutes.

important_dates.label: Application Deadline
important_dates.value: December 30, 2025 (already passed; check CDS II 2026 dates)

important_dates.label: Training Commencement
important_dates.value: January (CDS I) / July (CDS II) of following year

preparation_tips: CDS English is language-focused: grammar, vocabulary, comprehension, sentence structure. Study: Wren & Martin, practice synonyms/antonyms, reading comprehension passages, sentence correction. This is easier than competitive exams like UPSC.

General Knowledge covers static (history, geography, polity) and current affairs. Study: NCERT 6-12, monthly newspapers (6 months), monthly GK magazines. Focus: Indian military history, defence policies, current defence initiatives, geo-political developments. CDS GK is broader than banking exams but narrower than UPSC.

Mathematics is 'Elementary' (10th class level) — much easier than NDA. Topics: arithmetic, algebra, trigonometry (basic), geometry, mensuration, statistics. Don't over-prepare this; focus on speed + accuracy. Spend 30 mins daily.

SSB preparation is CRITICAL and must start BEFORE written exam results. Read about OLQs, practice group discussions daily, develop national awareness, improve communication skills, and build physical fitness. Many candidates clear written exam but fail SSB. Coaching institutes offer mock SSBs — take at least 3-4 mock sessions.

faqs.question: I have a non-engineering degree. Can I join the Air Force through CDS?
faqs.answer: Yes, through OTA. However, note: Air Force Academy (AFA) through CDS requires Physics + Maths at 12th level (or BE/BTech in any field). If your graduation didn't include Physics+Maths, you can't apply for AFA. But you CAN apply for IMA (Army) or OTA with any degree. Check your eligibility carefully.

faqs.question: Can women apply for CDS?
faqs.answer: Women can apply for OTA (since 2024, for the first time). IMA, Naval Academy, and Air Force Academy are currently for men only (as of 2026). Women can join the Air Force through AFCAT exam and Navy through specific women officer entries — check service websites for latest updates on gender-inclusive recruitment.

faqs.question: What is the expected CDS written exam cutoff?
faqs.answer: Cutoffs vary: IMA typically 110-130 out of 300, OTA 90-110 out of 200. These are relatively low (36-43% for IMA) — the real challenge is SSB. Focus on clearing the cutoff comfortably (150+/300 is safe), then prepare thoroughly for SSB.

faqs.question: OTA — can I leave after 14 years?
faqs.answer: Yes. OTA officers serve 14 years with option to extend. They can also apply for Permanent Commission conversion. After leaving, you receive gratuity + pension (if 20+ years of service). Many SSC officers transition to civil services, corporate careers, or further military service after their tenure.

faqs.question: How important is physical fitness for CDS?
faqs.answer: Very important. You must pass SSB physical tasks (5-km run in specified time, obstacle course, endurance tests) and military medical (vision 6/6 in better eye, 6/9 in worse eye with correction; no color blindness; weight proportionate to height). Start fitness training 6 months before SSB. Running 3-5 km daily is essential.

faqs.question: CDS salary — is it lower than banking?
faqs.answer: Starting salary is identical: ₹56,100 basic (₹70-90K in-hand with allowances). But banking exams like SBI PO start at ₹48,480 basic (₹80-82K in-hand including allowances). Military salary grows slower initially, but benefits (free quarters, medical, transport) add significant value. After 10 years, military pay is comparable to banking.

faqs.question: IMA officer vs IAS officer — which is better?
faqs.answer: Different roles: IAS = civilian administrator (highest power in governance, Cabinet Secretary level). IMA officer = military commander (Gen commanding armies, highest: Chief of Defence Staff). Salaries comparable, but IAS has more political influence; military has global authority. Choose: Civil governance (IAS) vs national defence (IMA).

syllabus.subject: Mathematics
syllabus.topics: Algebra, matrices & determinants, trigonometry, analytical geometry (2D & 3D), differential calculus, integral calculus, vector algebra, statistics & probability

syllabus.subject: General Ability Test (GAT)
syllabus.topics: English (grammar, vocabulary, comprehension), physics, chemistry, biology, history, geography, current affairs, general science

study_plan.tasks: NDA maths is 12th-level. Complete: algebra, trigonometry, calculus from NCERT 11-12. Science from NCERT 9-10. English vocabulary 20 words/day. Physical fitness training — NDA has SSB interview with physical tests.
History (modern India focus), geography (India + world), polity basics. Current affairs of last 1 year. Practice English comprehension daily. Mock tests for maths speed.
Full mocks weekly. If written exam clears, SSB interview preparation — personality tests, group discussions, interviews, physical tasks. Read about defence forces, current military operations.
